summary: AudioRecorderBottomSheet implements AC-12/AC-15/AC-19/AC-20 against the Gallery AudioRecorderPanel reference with correct AudioRecord lifecycle ordering (stopŌåÆrelease), idempotent finish() via AtomicBoolean CAS, DisposableEffect teardown, permission parity with CameraBottomSheet, and the ErrorLog 'audio' component that matches the whitelist enabled in core-runtime (D27). ChatViewModel.addAudio enforces MAX_AUDIO_CLIP_COUNT=1 defensively behind the disabled mic button, and test coverage for the pure helpers (formatTimer, isAudioDenialPermanent) + all VM paths (addAudio, no-op on duplicate, coexistence with images, send transfer, reportAudioError snackbar) is solid ŌĆö recording-flow tests are correctly deferred to manual smoke per tech-spec Testing Strategy / Task 3 + Task 8 precedent. No critical issues. Main concern: a narrow AC-19 race where a 30-sec auto-stop or Stop tap landing inside the ON_PAUSEŌåÆDisposableEffect.onDispose window can still save the buffer the user intended to drop ŌĆö a one-line `completed.set(true)` in LifecycleEventEffect closes it. Thread-safety of `RecorderHandle` mutable fields and cross-thread Compose state writes from the IO read loop are the other two major-but-non-blocking findings.

suggestions.suggestion: `LifecycleEventEffect(ON_PAUSE)` calls `onPauseDismiss()` only, which merely schedules `scope.launch { sheetState.hide(); onDismiss() }` on Main. Between that dispatch and `DisposableEffect.onDispose` actually firing (after `showAudioSheet=false` propagates and composition exits), the IO read loop keeps running ŌĆö it checks `recordingState == RECORDSTATE_RECORDING` only between `recorder.read()` blocking returns. During the hide-animation window (~150-300 ms), two paths can still commit audio: (a) the 30-sec auto-stop boundary inside `while (...)` fires ŌåÆ `finish()` ŌåÆ `onSave` ŌåÆ `addAudio`; (b) an in-flight Stop tap the user pressed milliseconds before the phone call interrupt races the same way. Either path violates AC-19 ("drop the half-finished buffer on call/background/lock"). The idempotency guard `completed: AtomicBoolean` is already in place ŌĆö set it preemptively inside the lifecycle effect: `completed.set(true); onPauseDismiss()`. That makes the subsequent `finish()` CAS fail and `onSave` never fires, leaving `DisposableEffect.onDispose` to release the native recorder. Probability is low per individual recording, but AC-19 is specifically the edge case this sheet exists to honour.
suggestions.benefit: Closes the one remaining AC-19 hole ŌĆö no audio attachment can sneak into the VM during a call/background/lock interrupt, even if the 30-sec boundary or a Stop tap lands in the animation window.

suggestions.suggestion: `RecorderHandle.recorder`, `job`, and `stream` are plain `var` fields written from the IO thread (inside the `LaunchedEffect`'s `scope.launch(Dispatchers.IO)` block: lines 205-208) and read from Main (Stop button handler at lines 282-284, `DisposableEffect.onDispose` at lines 233-241). The JMM gives no happens-before guarantee for plain `var` across threads, so in principle Main could observe a null `recorder` even after IO has assigned it, or a stale job reference after `finish`. In practice this works on ARM because the assignments happen inside coroutine suspension points that emit memory barriers, and `AudioRecord.getMinBufferSize` / constructor issue their own barriers ŌĆö but the contract is implicit. Clean fix: annotate the three fields `@Volatile` (cheapest), or hold them in `AtomicReference` (matches the existing `AtomicBoolean completed` style at line 184). Same fix removes the `runCatching { ... ?: return@runCatching }` double-check idiom at line 283 ŌĆö the field's atomicity is the guarantee, not the snapshot inside runCatching.
suggestions.benefit: Makes the cross-thread contract explicit and matches the `AtomicBoolean completed` pattern already in use for terminal-path idempotency.

suggestions.suggestion: `elapsedMs = now` (line 217) writes a Compose `mutableLongStateOf` from the IO read loop. Compose's global snapshot tolerates cross-thread state writes and posts the recomposition to Main, so this works ŌĆö but the convention in this codebase (and the camera sheet review flagged the same for `capturing = false`) is to mutate Compose state on Main. Wrap the two writes (`elapsedMs = now` and the pre-finish final clamp) with `withContext(Dispatchers.Main.immediate) { elapsedMs = now }`, or compute `elapsedMs` via a derived state off a `StateFlow<Long>` that the IO loop emits to. Cheapest option: after the loop exits, do one `withContext(Dispatchers.Main) { elapsedMs = finalMs }` before `finish()` ŌĆö the per-read-tick update then becomes best-effort. Note that Gallery's reference writes from IO too (line 262 of AudioRecorderPanel.kt), so this is strictly a codebase-style tightening, not a Gallery regression.
suggestions.benefit: Eliminates implicit reliance on Compose's cross-thread tolerance; matches the convention used elsewhere in the codebase for VM/UI state transitions.

suggestions.suggestion: `RecorderHandle.stream` field is set at line 207 but never read back ŌĆö the read loop captures `stream` as a local variable (line 206) and every subsequent reader uses that local. The dispose path at line 241 nulls `recorderRef.stream = null` but that's a no-op because nothing reads it. Drop the field from `RecorderHandle` (and the `stream = stream` assignment) to avoid misleading a future reader into thinking it's shared state; pass the stream into `finish()` via the closure (already does so via parameter). Minor, but the file KDoc at lines 298-300 calls this out as "Populated by the LaunchedEffect ... cleared by DisposableEffect.onDispose" which implies consumers exist.
suggestions.benefit: Removes dead state that implies multi-reader semantics where there are none; simplifies the RecorderHandle contract.

suggestions.suggestion: `audio_record_start` ("ąŚą░ą┐ąĖčüą░čéčī") and `audio_record_timer_format` ("%1$d čü") are declared but unreferenced ŌĆö the sheet's only action label is `audio_record_stop` (line 290 of AudioRecorderBottomSheet.kt), and the timer renders directly via `formatTimer(elapsedMs)` which produces "MM:SS" without going through a stringResource. Either wire `audio_record_timer_format` through (nice if the string ever needs localisation beyond `"%02d:%02d"`) or remove both unused keys to keep strings.xml lean. Similar observation for `attachment_audio_disabled` at line 74 ŌĆö the mic button is simply `enabled = false`, no snackbar is shown on tap, so the resource is unused. The user preference is terse / no ceremony, so removing unused keys is the cheaper fix.
suggestions.benefit: Keeps strings.xml grep-consistent ŌĆö every declared key has a caller.

suggestions.suggestion: `buildRecorder` has `@SuppressLint("MissingPermission")` and a good KDoc explaining that the caller gates the call behind a permission check. Consider also adding `@RequiresPermission(Manifest.permission.RECORD_AUDIO)` ŌĆö the pair is idiomatic in AndroidX: SuppressLint silences the lint warning at the call site, RequiresPermission documents the contract for callers and makes static analysis flag mis-use. Zero runtime cost.
suggestions.benefit: Self-documents the permission contract; helps lint catch a future caller who forgets to gate the call.

suggestions.suggestion: Same blank-sheet flash flagged in CameraBottomSheet review: while the OS permission dialog is up (between the `LaunchedEffect(Unit)` firing at line 145 and the launcher returning), `ModalBottomSheet` is already in composition with `hasPermission = false`, so the `if (hasPermission)` branch renders an empty sheet. Two fixes: (a) only render `ModalBottomSheet` when `hasPermission == true` (cleanest ŌĆö delays sheet animation until after permission grant); (b) render a small progress indicator in the empty branch. The CameraBottomSheet precedent accepted the flash; if the decision is to keep parity, leave as-is. If the task-9 author wants to address it for both sheets, it's a joint 3-line change.
suggestions.benefit: Removes the blank-sheet frame behind the OS permission dialog on first use; matches if decided jointly with CameraBottomSheet.

suggestions.suggestion: The UI renders "00:00" + red dot from the moment `AudioRecorderContent` composes ŌĆö but `recorder.startRecording()` only runs after `buildRecorder()` succeeds inside the `LaunchedEffect(Unit)` (line 211). Between composition-start and IO-thread `startRecording()`, the user sees a recording-UI without actual recording happening. Window is milliseconds on most devices but can be 100+ ms on slow OEMs. Cosmetic; fix would be a `var started by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }` flipped to true on the IO thread after `startRecording()` returns and conditionally showing the pulsing red indicator. Low priority ŌĆö the `LaunchedEffect` runs synchronously from Main's recompose loop, so the perceived delay is usually negligible.
suggestions.benefit: Polishes the first frame of the recording UI; matches the stated intent that the red dot means "actively recording".

suggestions.suggestion: `DisposableEffect(Unit)` keys on a constant. The same precedent comment from the CameraBottomSheet review applies ŌĆö if `LocalLifecycleOwner.current` or `scope` ever changes under this composable (nested NavHost, custom Compose host), the effect won't re-run and the recorder would stay bound to the stale handles. Key on `scope` (since `scope` is the one that ownes the job lifetime): `DisposableEffect(scope)`. Current Phase-2 topology never triggers this, and Gallery keys on Unit too, so it's purely a robustness nit. Flag for consistency with the note left on task 8.
suggestions.benefit: Future-proofs against nested-host scenarios; matches the Task 8 precedent note.

suggestions.suggestion: `animateAndDismiss` is defined inline inside `AudioRecorderBottomSheet` (line 121) and also effectively duplicated in `CameraBottomSheet` (line 117). Pure helper, same shape ŌĆö extract to a small internal file `BottomSheetDismiss.kt` in the same package: `internal fun CoroutineScope.animateAndDismiss(sheetState: SheetState, onDismiss: () -> Unit)`. Task 11 plans "shared sheet host state" per tech-spec, so the extraction has a downstream home. Not in scope for Task 9; flag as foreseeable cleanup. Matches the task-8 note about `showCameraSheet` / `showAudioSheet` becoming an `ActiveSheet` sealed class.
suggestions.benefit: Reduces duplicated sheet-dismiss logic and preps for Task 11 consolidation.

suggestions.suggestion: `runCatching { val r = recorderRef.recorder ?: return@runCatching; if (r.recordingState == AudioRecord.RECORDSTATE_RECORDING) r.stop() }` reads as a defensive box around two operations that in practice don't throw under normal lifecycle: `recorder` access is a field read, `recordingState` / `stop()` are documented to be safe on an initialised AudioRecord. The runCatching is probably there to absorb `IllegalStateException` from a stop() called on a recorder already released by `onDispose` (race). That's plausible; add a one-line comment explaining which exception is being absorbed so a future reader doesn't tighten this to a specific catch or remove the guard entirely.
suggestions.benefit: Makes the implicit race documentation explicit; matches the already-excellent KDoc comments elsewhere in the file.

suggestions.suggestion: The class KDoc honestly documents that `stopButtonSavesByteArrayToViewModel`, `onPauseReleasesAndDismisses`, and `stateNotInitializedShowsSnackbarAndDismisses` are covered by manual smoke on Honor 200 ŌĆö consistent with Task 3 / Task 8 precedent and tech-spec ┬¦ Testing Strategy. No action needed. As with the camera sheet review, a class-level `// TODO: cover in androidTest when Compose UI tests land` marker would make the deferred work discoverable via grep rather than by remembering to read the KDoc. Purely discoverability.
suggestions.benefit: Makes the deferred Compose-UI coverage searchable by convention (matches the feedback left on AudioRecorderBottomSheetTest's sibling CameraBottomSheetTest).

suggestions.suggestion: `addAudio` silently no-ops when an audio attachment already exists (line 270-272). The KDoc at line 263-266 calls this out as a defensive TOCTOU guard behind the disabled mic button ŌĆö good. Consider symmetry with `addImages` / `addImageBitmap`: those emit `R.string.attachment_max_images_reached` when the cap is hit. The audio-cap equivalent `R.string.attachment_audio_disabled` already exists (strings.xml line 74) but is currently unused (see separate finding on strings.xml). Either wire a snackbar here for diagnostic parity with images ŌĆö the user presumably did something (rapid tap, stale UI) to get past the disabled mic ŌĆö or accept the asymmetry because the UI gate is stronger for audio (a `!hasAudioAttachment` enable flag on the button vs the images path which only clips post-decode). Non-blocking; the KDoc documents the intent.
suggestions.benefit: Aligns the audio-cap surface behaviour with the images-cap surface, or explicitly documents why they diverge.
